title: hieracium atratum subsp. parabolicum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. parabolicum is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, characterized by its distinctive parabolic leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Hieracium atratum subsp. parabolicum include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and potential antioxidant properties. Hierbal preparations of this plant often involve drying the leaves and flowers to create a tea or infusion, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
